sourceRef: tip:When to certify and when to wait

slides.headline: Timing: When to Certify and When to Wait
slides.body: Understand the timing rule to avoid overpayments.

slides.headline: Understand the 85/15 Rule
slides.body: The 85/15 rule is a key threshold for certifying.
bullets: 85% of student aid is covered by the GI Bill.
15% is covered by the Yellow Ribbon Program.

slides.headline: Check Your Student's Status
slides.body: Ensure the student is 100% eligible for aid.
bullets: Verify the student is not in default.
Confirm the student is not in a period of non-eligibility.

slides.headline: Review Chapter 33 Eligibility
slides.body: Confirm the student meets Chapter 33 requirements.
bullets: Check for eligibility based on service type.

slides.headline: Wait for VA Approval
slides.body: Wait for VA approval before certifying if unsure.
bullets: Hold certification if you're not certain of the student's eligibility.
Consult with your SAA for guidance.

slides.headline: Certify with Confidence
slides.body: Certify once you have verified all eligibility criteria.
bullets: Certify after receiving final VA approval.
Keep copies of all supporting documentation.

slides.body: Learn more about certifying student veterans with VASCO Assistant Pro.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Master the timing rule to avoid overpayments and ensure compliance. Learn the steps to certify and wait effectively with VASCO Assistant Pro. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 3, as it could benefit from a more concise headline to better summarize the content.
